citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
An umbrella cockatoo has missing feathers and a full thickness almond-shaped wound approximately one inch in length
on its right breast area. The exposed tissue feels dry and papery, is not apparently painful to the touch, and does not
appear inflamed or infected. The facility stated that the bird has exhibited self-injurious behaviors since they acquired the
bird six years ago. While the animal’s self-injurious behaviors have improved overall, this wound will recurrently scab or
start to heal over and then recur. The program of veterinary care does not address wound care. The facility stated that
they have discussed the self-injurious behavior with the attending veterinarian (AV) within the last four months but had not
specifically contacted the AV when the bird had most recently removed the scab/healing skin. The AV stated he was not
aware that this was a cyclical issue.
Self-injurious behavior in birds may indicate an underlying psychological or medical condition. Veterinary assessment is
crucial to evaluating medical diseases or environmental stress. Veterinary assessment is also valuable when evaluating
the environmental enhancement plan for the special considerations birds with psychological distress need.
Communicating updates on changes to chronic and recurring conditions may provide the veterinarian information that
guides assessment and treatment recommendations.
The licensee must ensure that thorough daily observations are conducted and continued timely and accurate
communication regarding problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being are conveyed to the attending veterinarian.
Correct by April 20, 2026

The facility has not maintained records which fully and correctly disclose the following information concerning animals in
their possession or animals otherwise disposed of by the exhibitor. Missing information includes the name and address of
the person from whom the animals were purchased or otherwise acquired, the name of address of the person to whom an
animal was sold or given, and the date of acquisition (i.e. purchase date or birth date if born on the property). The
incomplete records were observed for a ring-tail lemur, hybrid macaw, domestic goats and sheep, red deer, north
American porcupine, and wolves.
Appropriate documentation of regulated animals is essential for proper identification, to trace and track animals in case of
disease outbreak, and to ensure all parties that are required to be licensed have a current, valid license.
Every dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly disclose the following information
concerning animals, other than dogs and cats, purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, leased, or otherwise in his
or her possession or under his or her control, or which is transported, sold, euthanized, or otherwise disposed of by that
dealer or exhibitor. The records shall also include any offspring of any animal while in his or her possession or under his
or her control.
Correct by May 7, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
An umbrella cockatoo has missing feathers and a full thickness almond-shaped wound approximately one inch in length 
on its right breast area. The exposed tissue feels dry and papery, is not apparently painful to the touch, and does not 
appear inflamed or infected. The facility stated that the bird has exhibited self-injurious behaviors since they acquired the 
bird six years ago. While the animal’s self-injurious behaviors have improved overall, this wound will recurrently scab or 
start to heal over and then recur. The program of veterinary care does not address wound care. The facility stated that 
they have discussed the self-injurious behavior with the attending veterinarian (AV) within the last four months but had not 
specifically contacted the AV when the bird had most recently removed the scab/healing skin. The AV stated he was not 
aware that this was a cyclical issue.  
 
Self-injurious behavior in birds may indicate an underlying psychological or medical condition. Veterinary assessment is 
crucial to evaluating medical diseases or environmental stress. Veterinary assessment is also valuable when evaluating 
the environmental enhancement plan for the special considerations birds with psychological distress need. 
Communicating updates on changes to chronic and recurring conditions may provide the veterinarian information that 
guides assessment and treatment recommendations.  
 
The licensee must ensure that thorough daily observations are conducted and continued timely and accurate 
communication regarding problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being are conveyed to the attending veterinarian.  
 
Correct by April 20, 2026 
2.75(b)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
The facility has not maintained records which fully and correctly disclose the following information concerning animals in 
their possession or animals otherwise disposed of by the exhibitor. Missing information includes the name and address of 
the person from whom the animals were purchased or otherwise acquired, the name of address of the person to whom an 
animal was sold or given, and the date of acquisition (i.e. purchase date or birth date if born on the property). The 
incomplete records were observed for a ring-tail lemur, hybrid macaw, domestic goats and sheep, red deer, north 
American porcupine, and wolves.

Appropriate documentation of regulated animals is essential for proper identification, to trace and track animals in case of 
disease outbreak, and to ensure all parties that are required to be licensed have a current, valid license.  
 
Every dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly disclose the following information 
concerning animals, other than dogs and cats, purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, leased, or otherwise in his 
or her possession or under his or her control, or which is transported, sold, euthanized, or otherwise disposed of by that 
dealer or exhibitor. The records shall also include any offspring of any animal while in his or her possession or under his 
or her control. 
 
Correct by May 7, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
